articles.title: Border districts of Rajasthan reeling under severe heatwave - News On AIR
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Rajasthan
articles.full_text:
The border districts of Rajasthan are currently reeling under severe heatwave conditions. Temperatures at several places are being recorded between 45 and 47 degrees Celsius. Barmer was the hottest place in the state today, with the maximum temperature recorded at 48 point 3 degrees Celsius. Phalodi registered a temperature of 46 point 4 degrees Celsius. Besides this, temperatures crossed 45 degrees Celsius in Jaisalmer, Bikaner and Chittorgarh, while several other places recorded temperatures above 44 degrees Celsius.
The Director of the Meteorological Centre Jaipur told Akashvani that severe heatwave conditions are likely to continue in the western districts of the state for the next three to four days.
Meanwhile, north-eastern districts of the state witnessed rainfall during the afternoon. The showers provided slight relief to people from the intense heat.

articles.full_text:
Scorching Rajasthan: Rising Temperatures Trigger Heatwave Alert
Rajasthan is experiencing intense heatwave conditions, with temperatures peaking at 48.3 degrees Celsius in Barmer. The meteorological department forecasts a rise in both maximum and minimum temperatures statewide. Major cities, including Jaipur, recorded temperatures above 41 degrees, and further heatwaves are expected in Jodhpur and Bikaner.
Heatwave conditions intensified across Rajasthan on Tuesday as parts of the state witnessed a soaring rise in temperatures. The Meteorological Department has predicted that upcoming days will see a marginal increase in both maximum and minimum temperatures.
Barmer emerged as the hottest area, registering a sweltering 48.3 degrees Celsius. Other areas such as Phalodi reached 46.4 degrees, followed by Jaisalmer at 45.8 degrees, Chittorgarh with 45.6 degrees, and Bikaner at 45 degrees. Jaipur, the state's capital, wasn't spared either, recording a peak of 43.2 degrees.
These extreme conditions suggest a looming heatwave threat for Jodhpur and Bikaner divisions, as temperatures across all major cities surpassed the 41 degrees mark. Residents are advised to take necessary precautions as the state braces for the intensified heat.
(With inputs from agencies.)
- weather

articles.title: Heatwave conditions in Rajasthan; mercury to soar further, says MeT dept - newsdrum.in
articles.full_text:
Jaipur, May 12 (PTI) Heatwave conditions prevailed in parts of Rajasthan on Tuesday as the MeT department said most parts of the state will see a slight increase in maximum and minimum temperatures in the coming days.
Barmer was recorded as the hottest place in Rajasthan with the maximum temperature of 48.3 degrees Celsius.
According to the MeT department, Phalodi recorded 46.4 degrees, followed by 45.8 degrees in Jaisalmer, 45.6 degrees in Chittorgarh and 45 degrees in Bikaner.
State capital Jaipur recorded 43.2 degrees. All major cities across the state recorded maximum temperature above 41 degrees, the department said.
Jodhpur and Bikaner divisions are likely to witness heatwaves in the coming days, it added. PTI AG PRK

articles.title: Barmer hottest at 47.3 degrees C, heatwave and thunderstorm alerts issued across Rajasthan - Social News XYZ
articles.full_text:
Jaipur, May 12 (SocialNews.XYZ) Rajasthan continued to reel under extreme and sharply contrasting weather conditions as Barmer emerged as the hottest place in the country, recording a maximum temperature of 47.3 degrees Celsius, the highest in the state so far this season.
The Meteorological Department has now issued heatwave and thunderstorm alerts across multiple districts, warning that the dramatic weather swings are likely to continue over the next three days. There is a possibility of a slight rise in both minimum and maximum temperatures across most parts of Rajasthan over the next few days.
The Meteorological Department has warned of heatwave conditions and hot nights in parts of the Jodhpur and Bikaner divisions for the next 4-5 days.
In the south-eastern districts as well, maximum temperatures are likely to remain between 43 and 45 degrees C in the coming days, with the possibility of heatwave conditions and unusually warm nights at some places.
Meanwhile, during the next 48 hours, thunderstorms, strong winds blowing at speeds of 40-50 kmph, and a drizzle are likely in parts of the Bikaner division, the Shekhawati region, Jaipur, and northern areas of the Bharatpur division. Except for northern Rajasthan, weather conditions remained mostly clear across the state over the last 24 hours.
Districts including Bikaner, Jaisalmer, Phalodi, Barmer, Jodhpur, and Jalore experienced intense heatwave conditions. While Barmer recorded a maximum temperature of 47.3 degrees Celsius, the highest this season, Jaisalmer, Phalodi, Bikaner, Jodhpur, and Chittorgarh also recorded temperatures above 44 degrees Celsius.
Meteorological experts say hot winds entering the Thar region from Pakistan without obstruction are a major reason behind the sharp rise in temperatures in Jaisalmer and Barmer. Parts of Sri Ganganagar and Hanumangarh witnessed thunderstorms, rain, and hailstorms on Monday.
Cloud cover persisted since morning, followed by strong winds and light rainfall at several places. Ganganagar recorded a maximum temperature of 42.8 degrees Celsius, while Hanumangarh recorded 38 degrees Celsius.
The capital city, Jaipur, recorded a rise in temperature on Monday, with the maximum temperature touching 39.9 degrees Celsius. Bright sunshine and humidity made conditions uncomfortable throughout the day.
The Meteorological Department has issued a yellow alert for thunderstorms and rain in Jaipur for Tuesday. Udaipur experienced intense heat, with warm winds continuing even during the night.
On Monday, the maximum temperature reached 42.3 degrees Celsius, while the minimum temperature was recorded at 25.9 degrees C. Night temperatures in Kota rose by nearly 2 degrees to touch 42.7 degrees C.
Residents faced scorching sunshine and hot winds throughout the afternoon. The minimum temperature stood at 27.4 degrees Celsius. A yellow alert for heatwave conditions has been issued for Tuesday.
Source: IANS
